post.summary: Search on Skills, Occupations, ISCED-F and ISCO concepts
post.description: Get a list of concepts matching the provided filters

get.summary: Get a skill by its identifier

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The namespace of the ID that follows

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier associated with the resource to be fetched

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description: The default language of the returned response

parameters.name: Accept-Header
parameters.description: The default accept header of the returned response

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The language of the returned response (overwrites the Accept-Language header). Language "en-us" is only available for ESCO version "v1.0.9" and language "uk" is available for ESCO version "v1.1.0" or later.

parameters.name: selectedVersion
parameters.description: The selected ESCO dataset version

get.summary: Get the related resources of a given skill by a given relation.
get.description: Returns a list view of related resources in the given concept scheme

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The namespace of the ID that follows

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier associated with the resource to be fetched

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: The relation to get the related resources for

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description: The default language of the returned response

parameters.name: inScheme
parameters.description: The concept scheme to look for related resources

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of the returned resources in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset of the returned resources in the response

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The language of the returned response (overwrites the Accept-Language header). Language "en-us" is only available for ESCO version "v1.0.9" and language "uk" is available for ESCO version "v1.1.0" or later.

parameters.name: selectedVersion
parameters.description: The selected ESCO dataset version

get.summary: Get the converted skills of the given skill (if any)

get.summary: Get all resources of a given class matching a plain text in a given language.
get.description: This is a full text search on the preferred labels, the alternative labels, the hidden labels, and the description of a resource. The search must be scoped on class type and language. The search result is paged.

parameters.name: Accept-Language
parameters.description: The default language of the returned response

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to search for

parameters.name: hasLabel
parameters.description: The label property to search for

parameters.name: hasLabelRole
parameters.description: Allows filtering on gender specific terms

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The class of resources to search for

parameters.name: inScheme
parameters.description: The concept scheme to search for resources that are contained in it

parameters.name: facet
parameters.description: The facet to count for. The response will return for each different value of the given facet the number of occurrences in the search result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of the returned resources in the response

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset of the returned resources in the response

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The language of the returned response (overwrites the Accept-Language header). Language "en-us" is only available for ESCO version "v1.0.9" and language "uk" is available for ESCO version "v1.1.0" or later.

parameters.name: selectedVersion
parameters.description: The selected ESCO dataset version

parameters.name: viewObsolete
parameters.description: To include (or not) obsoleted concepts

get.summary: Representation of skill groups share
get.description: Get the skill groups representation for related and optional skill/competences of the given occupation

items.example: ["trainer in fire academy","fire instructor","firefighter teaching practitioner","educator of firefighters","firefighter trainer","instructor in fire academy","fire academy instructor","fire academy trainer","instructor of firefighters","trainer of firefighters"]

description.example: Firefighter instructors train probationary, new academy recruits, or cadets, on the theory and practice necessary to become a firefighter. They conduct theoretical lectures on academic subjects such as law, basic chemistry, safety regulations, risk management, fire prevention, reading blueprints etc. Fire academy instructors also provide more hands-on, practical instruction regarding the usage of assistive equipment and rescue tools such as a fire hose, fire axe, smoke mask etc., but also heavy physical training, breathing techniques, first aid, self defense tactics and vehicle operations. They also prepare and develop lesson plans and new training programmes as new public service-related regulations and issues arise. The instructors monitor the students' progress, evaluate them individually and prepare performance evaluation reports.
